title: hoya cumingiana subsp. catanauanensis
intro: Hoya cumingiana subsp. catanauanensis, also known as the Catanduanes waxflower, is a flowering plant species in the family Apocynaceae, native to the Philippines. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions or decoctions, and it is often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
